questions.question: What is the primary goal of color smoothing in image processing?
options: A) To enhance contrast and details in an image
B) To reduce the impact of noise in an image
C) To change the color of an image
D) To add a texture to an image
questions.explanation: Color smoothing helps to reduce the impact of noise in an image, making it look more uniform and stable.

questions.question: What is the purpose of color sharpening in image processing?
options: A) To reduce the impact of noise in an image
B) To enhance the details of an image
C) To change the color of an image
D) To add a texture to an image
questions.explanation: Color sharpening is used to enhance the details of an image by amplifying the high-frequency components, making the image look sharper and more detailed.

questions.question: What is pseudocolor image processing used for?
options: A) To change the color of an image
B) To enhance the contrast of an image
C) To reduce the impact of noise in an image
D) To add a texture to an image
questions.explanation: Pseudocolor image processing is used to change the color of an image by assigning a color to each pixel based on its intensity value, making the image easier to visualize.

questions.question: Which color model is commonly used in image segmentation?
options: A) RGB
B) HSV
C) YCbCr
D) CMYK
questions.explanation: YCbCr (Luminance-Chrominance) color model is commonly used in image segmentation as it separates the luminance (brightness) from the chrominance (color) information, making it easier to segment the image.

questions.question: What type of noise can be present in color images?
options: A) Gaussian noise
B) Salt and pepper noise
C) Shot noise
D) All of the above
questions.explanation: Color images can be affected by various types of noise, including Gaussian noise, salt and pepper noise, and shot noise, which can degrade the quality of the image.
